text: Onboarding friction drops when the progress marker stays visible across steps. This should stay visible in activity feed for quick triage. When users see consistent progress markers, they are less likely to lose track of their workflow, which improves overall satisfaction. Additionally, keeping this visible helps new users understand the flow more intuitively, reducing onboarding time and confusion.
Team found cluster of duplicate synthesis notes during handoff review. Follow-up needed before next stakeholder review.

Workshop participants reacted faster to examples than to abstract prompts. Worth highlighting in details panel once selection state lands. This insight suggests that concrete examples should be prioritized in future workshops. Participants also reported higher engagement when examples were visually distinct, indicating a need for better visual cues in the UI.
Reviewers requested clearer ownership tags before next planning cycle. Could become default example for sorting behavior.
Recent feedback highlights confusion around invite permissions. Useful seed note for pagination and count verification.
Users kept scrolling past status updates until timestamps became more prominent. Pairs well with author and color filters in tests.
Affinity mapping surfaced stronger alignment after lane labels were simplified. Timestamp order matters because nearby events read as single narrative.
Cross-functional review flagged need for smaller batches in activity summaries. Should help exercise realistic copy lengths in feed cards.
Board cleanup pass reduced noise before leadership playback. This should stay visible in activity feed for quick triage.
Research playback showed observers value chronological context over raw volume. Follow-up needed before next stakeholder review.

QA noted repeated edge cases when multiple authors post in same minute. Worth highlighting in details panel once selection state lands. These edge cases can complicate activity tracking and may require additional logic to ensure accurate sorting and filtering. Addressing this will improve reliability for teams with high activity rates.
Support themes suggest color labels should reinforce visual note cues. Could become default example for sorting behavior.
Sprint retro notes emphasize keeping filters obvious on smaller screens. Useful seed note for pagination and count verification.

Discovery session produced enough follow-up threads to justify incremental loading. Pairs well with author and color filters in tests. Incremental loading not only improves performance but also enhances the user experience by reducing wait times. This approach is especially beneficial for boards with a large volume of notes and frequent updates.

Facilitators want easier ways to spot newest notes without losing context. Timestamp order matters because nearby events read as single narrative. Providing visual indicators for new notes and grouping related events can help facilitators maintain context and streamline their review process. This feature is highly requested in feedback sessions.
